steps.title: Use total sums, not averages alone
steps.content:
The total of all $180$ boiling points is:
$$180\cdot164$$
The total for the $60$ semivolatile compounds is:
$$60\cdot304$$

steps.title: Subtract to get the volatile total
steps.content:
The remaining $120$ compounds are volatile, so their total boiling point is:
$$180\cdot164-60\cdot304=11,280$$

steps.title: Divide by the 120 volatile compounds
steps.content:
Mean equals total divided by count:
$$\frac{11,280}{120}=94$$
The mean boiling point of the volatile compounds is 94 degrees Celsius.
